findings.evidence: New file lines 49-62 contain only the two `noncomputable abbrev` bodies and no lemma declarations; the analogous generic API has `@[simp] normalizerQuotientEquivMap_mk`/`_symm_mk` at `TauCeti/Algebra/Group/NormalizerQuotientConjugation.lean:128-143`, and the deck-specific wrapper restates them at `TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/UniversalCover/Deck/NormalizerQuotientConjugation.lean:54-97`.
findings.fix: Make the wrappers opaque `def`s where possible and add the named API: a `mem_basepointChangeSubgroup`/`*_iff` lemma and `[simp]` forward and inverse `basepointChangeNormalizerQuotientEquiv_*_mk` representative lemmas, plus the coe form if needed by downstream cover bookkeeping.
findings.issue: `basepointChangeSubgroup` and `basepointChangeNormalizerQuotientEquiv` are public `abbrev`s with no own `mem_*` or representative `[simp]` lemmas; the docstring explicitly points users to generic lemmas, exposing the body as the API.

summary: The new basepoint-change API is only reducible wrappers, so consumers must unfold the definitions instead of rewriting with stable characteristic lemmas under the new names.
